title: A couple of possibly dumb questions
display_title: A couple of possibly dumb questions
wikitext:
[]: A couple of possibly dumb questions.             Quote Post by neo-dragon » Sat Dec 03, 2011 2:30 pm Hey guys. I'm new here, but not new to NGE. That being said, I've recently been re-watching the series for the first time in several years, and I figured it's finally time to get the bottom of a couple of minor questions that bug me. 1. Did regular people (ie. outside of Nerv, Seele, and higher-ups in the U.N.) know about Angels before the 3rd attacked? If so, how? The existence of Adam, Lilith, and the Dead Sea Scrolls were obviously top secret, and the public thought that Second Impact was was caused by a meteorite. So what did people think the point of a fortress city like Tokyo-3 was? No one even seemed surprised by the appearance of strange monsters or giant robots fighting them. Also, don't people wonder why, with the exception of the ocean battle, Angels always attack one city? 2. I've always been confused by the LCL in the entry plugs. The pilots are always submerged in it, right? But there's seldom any sign that they are in a liquid medium. Usually no bubbles when they breathe, their voices aren't distorted, in episode 23 Rei even sheds visible tears which fall onto her lap, which obviously shouldn't be possible if she's submerged in liquid. Is this artistic licence or what? Anyway, sorry if these issues have already been discussed to death

[]: Re: A couple of possibly dumb questions.             Quote Post by liquidus118 » Sat Dec 03, 2011 3:38 pm 1. They didn't know until Sachiel attacked. Presumably Seele or the UN tried to keep it hushed up or something. And I think people were scared. Everyone left the city after the first couple of attacks. By the end the only people in Tokyo-3 are NERV staff. 2. They're just artistic license, or in the case of episode 23 Anno or Gainax fucking up.

[]: Re: A couple of possibly dumb questions.             Quote Post by KnightmareX13 » Sat Dec 03, 2011 3:47 pm neo-dragon wrote: Hey guys. I'm new here, but not new to NGE. That being said, I've recently been re-watching the series for the first time in several years, and I figured it's finally time to get the bottom of a couple of minor questions that bug me. 1. Did regular people (ie. outside of Nerv, Seele, and higher-ups in the U.N.) know about Angels before the 3rd attacked? If so, how? The existence of Adam, Lilith, and the Dead Sea Scrolls were obviously top secret, and the public thought that Second Impact was was caused by a meteorite. So what did people think the point of a fortress city like Tokyo-3 was? No one even seemed surprised by the appearance of strange monsters or giant robots fighting them. Also, don't people wonder why, with the exception of the ocean battle, Angels always attack one city? 2. I've always been confused by the LCL in the entry plugs. The pilots are always submerged in it, right? But there's seldom any sign that they are in a liquid medium. Usually no bubbles when they breathe, their voices aren't distorted, in episode 23 Rei even sheds visible tears which fall onto her lap, which obviously shouldn't be possible if she's submerged in liquid. Is this artistic licence or what? Anyway, sorry if these issues have already been discussed to death

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Sat Dec 03, 2011 3:50 pm Actually, my theory for the tear is that, since the tear is salty, it would have a higher density then the LCL, therefore falling to her lap. Although, it wouldn't explain why it wouldn't dissolve with the LCL before it hit her lap. Also, because salt water is actually a good conductor of electricity, it amuses me that the charges wouldn't actually zap Rei.

[]:             Quote Post by KnightmareX13 » Sat Dec 03, 2011 4:10 pm Stryker wrote: Actually, my theory for the tear is that, since the tear is salty, it would have a higher density then the LCL, therefore falling to her lap. Although, it wouldn't explain why it wouldn't dissolve with the LCL before it hit her lap. Also, because salt water is actually a good conductor of electricity, it amuses me that the charges wouldn't actually zap Rei.

[]: Re: A couple of possibly dumb questions.             Quote Post by Sachi » Sat Dec 03, 2011 4:58 pm KnightmareX13 wrote: 1. Probably not many civilians knew. The members of the Katsuragi expedition knew about the angels since they studied Adam and Dr. Katsuragi proposed the S 2 theory, proposing the existence of the S 2 engine which was discovered to be the power source behind the angels. It could be possible one of the members leaked info. Most of 2nd Impact deaths were caused by the raised water levels, so the cover story for the geofront could be a shelter from rogue waves. And the existence of the Eva's was kept top secret prior to Sachiel's attack.

[]:             Quote Post by neo-dragon » Sat Dec 03, 2011 5:47 pm Stryker wrote: Actually, my theory for the tear is that, since the tear is salty, it would have a higher density then the LCL, therefore falling to her lap. Although, it wouldn't explain why it wouldn't dissolve with the LCL before it hit her lap.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Dec 03, 2011 5:53 pm It's Japan. They've been having to deal with kaiju since Godzilla in the '50s, if not before.

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Sat Dec 03, 2011 6:54 pm It's not to much of a stretch for the civilians to believe that the angels were some sort of robot or mech.

[]:             Quote Post by neo-dragon » Sat Dec 03, 2011 8:34 pm Nothing in the series suggests that they were led to believe that, and even so, they should still wonder who constructed them and why.

[]:             Quote Post by arkiel » Sun Dec 04, 2011 12:39 am 1. It isn't that clear civilians knew what was going on AFTER the 3rd attacked. Everything seemed pretty hush-hush. We don't really know if anyone outside of SEELE and Tokyo-3 knew about Eva or the Angels before the 3rd. Afterwards though, it seemed to become 'common' classified knowledge for governments and big corporations like the one that built Jet-Alone. 2. My physics is a few years rusty, but I seem to recall that you can alter the perceived color of a substance with the correct wavelength of light. It could be that a property of the LCL allowed it to go clear, or that the display is false-colored to give accurate colors when viewed through the orange-yellow LCL medium. Somebody mentioned Rei's tears. Maybe the LCL was ejected in an attempt to slow the rate of biological contamination...? Claiming that its artistic license is all well and good, but empirical speculation really isn't even that hard, yo.

[]:             Quote Post by neo-dragon » Sun Dec 04, 2011 1:03 am The colour isn't the biggest issue by far, which is why I didn't even mention it. Like I said, it's things like sound and motion that don't fit. Rei's tears, the lack of bubbles, audible gasps and sighs that aren't liquidy, the motion of the pilot's hair, etc. None of it fits. I also found it a bit weird that when Toji and Kensuke entered the plug they commented on it being wet inside, but neither of them had any problem with the fact that they were actually breathing a liquid, something which no one could take to naturally, as it goes against human instincts and reflexes. No one even explained it to them. I know that Kensuke usually knows more about the Evas and Nerv than Shinji does, but I doubt that Toji knew what to expect. So as far as I can tell, it's either: a. Artistic license b. Lazy animation (or the animators have never been swimming or even taken a bath in their lives, to know how things behave in liquid) c. LCL is not always present

[]:             Quote Post by moneenerd » Sun Dec 04, 2011 1:32 am EVANGELION is riddled with plot inconsistencies, no doubt about it. We could go on for pages on all the things that don't add up. You would think that after sitting in a cockpit full of LCL, one would come out drenched in it right? Especially when in plain clothes right? Luckily, EVANGELION has a million redeeming qualities for every negative one. Nothing is perfect.

[]: Re: A couple of possibly dumb questions.             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sun Dec 04, 2011 7:48 am liquidus118 wrote: 2. They're just artistic license, or in the case of episode 23 Anno or Gainax fucking up.

[]: Re: A couple of possibly dumb questions.             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Dec 04, 2011 8:41 am FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: I don't remember there being any missing fluids in that episode...

[]: Re: A couple of possibly dumb questions.             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sun Dec 04, 2011 6:18 pm Mr. Tines wrote: Rei: These are tears. It's me who is crying.

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Sun Dec 04, 2011 9:10 pm In the unlikely event that we ever do see LAEM, I wonder how they'll deal with the cockpit LCL issue. Probably just jettison the concept. Still, I think it would be a cool visual to see the pilots' hair and whatnot reacting to it.

[]:             Quote Post by Xacebans » Mon Dec 05, 2011 7:13 pm Finally someone besides me complains about how the LCL's presence is in no way accounted for in like, any of the animation!

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Mon Dec 05, 2011 7:24 pm There are bubbles when the entry plug is first flooded with LCL (e.g. Shinji's first scene in an entry plug), that's because once you've evacuated your lungs of air, it's filled with LCL, it's not like your lungs keep generating bubbles afterwards. There's also animation from when the LCL in the entry plug gets dirty when Shinji is trapped in Leliel.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Mon Dec 05, 2011 7:51 pm The LCL is also visible when Ramiel boils it. I think part of it is a budgetary/animation quality thing. Asuka's hair was whipping around pretty fluidly during the EoE fight.
